key_insight: PNOT reconstructs divertor temperature fields by combining graph-based boundary physics, neural operator aggregation, and derivative-aware regularization to improve accuracy and physical consistency.
engineering_relevance: This enables near real-time thermal field prediction for tungsten monoblock divertors on EAST, supporting safer control and reduced melting risk in fusion operation.
method: Models boundary heat-flux relations as a structured graph
Uses graph attention to capture spatial physical dependencies
Adds a physics-aware neural operator with slicing plus Sobolev regularization
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that experimental results show improved prediction accuracy while preserving physical consistency compared with baseline approaches.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the physics priors and graph construction, so poor boundary-condition encoding could limit generalization.
paper_title: Temperature Field Reconstruction of Tungsten Monoblock Divertor on EAST using Physics-aware Neural Operator Transformer
